title: Music Mogul AI brings automation to the tour-booking process - Music Ally
text:
If you were ranking music-industry sectors by how much they’re driven by humans interacting with other humans, tour booking would surely come high up the list.
Nevertheless, this sector is not immune from startups offering AI-powered services.
Music Mogul AI
was founded by a booking agent, Brad Stewart, to automate some of the key tasks involved in booking and promoting tours.
The Los Angeles Times has
profiled the company
, describing its features “from identifying venues and emailing promoters to negotiating fees, advancing shows and marketing concerts”, including Stewart’s reassurance that this isn’t about putting human agents out of work.
Instead, it’s about extending the services they offer to artists lower down the live pyramid. “For an agency to take on an artist full time, they usually need to be grossing at least $200,000 a year in live revenue,” as he put it.
A similar argument has been used for tools like AI mastering and mixing, as well as the growing ranks of ‘AI managers’ – which are mainly release-plan generators.
“I didn’t build this because I think agents are obsolete,” added Stewart. “I built it because the system doesn’t work for a huge number of artists any more.”
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
